AI Experience & Platform Enabler
Prepared by Anirban Ghosal · Independent source-linked navigator
WhyAI stackUse casesModelWorkflow90 daysSources
Technology business case · AI-specific version

AI Experience & Platform Enabler Team

A Technology-owned capability to make enterprise AI useful in daily work: approved tools, Copilot experiences, agents, prompts, knowledge grounding, integrations, evaluation, support and measurable adoption.

🧠 AI product catalogue 🤖 Agent factory 🧩 MCP / connector layer 📚 Knowledge grounding ✅ Evaluation and quality
This is not a security team with AI controls. It is an AI platform, product and employee experience team — with security built in as a guardrail.
What changed in this version

The centre of gravity is now AI enablement

The previous page was too security-heavy. This version puts the AI work back in the centre: tooling, agent design, prompt patterns, workflows, knowledge, connectors, quality evaluation and adoption engineering.

70% AI enablement · 20% operations · 10% controls

Security, privacy and risk remain important, but they support the AI platform. They do not define the whole team.

Blunt diagnosis: If the page reads like security work, leaders will ask why this belongs in an AI team. The stronger story is that Technology needs an AI experience and platform function because business teams can identify value, but they usually do not own Copilot design patterns, agent publishing, prompt libraries, AI service support, knowledge grounding, connector architecture, evaluation, usage telemetry or reusable platform standards.
Business case

Why Technology needs this capability

AI is moving from experimentation to an everyday employee technology layer. That needs a platform owner, not just a use-case team.

🧠

AI is now a work interface

Copilot, agents and AI assistants sit inside daily work tools such as Teams, Outlook, Word, browsers, search and service portals.

🤖

Agents need lifecycle ownership

Microsoft documents agent creation, knowledge, tools, prompts, agent flows and workflows. Someone must operate this as a platform, not as random pilots.

🧩

AI needs integration patterns

MCP and connectors let AI apps connect to external data, tools and workflows. That creates a new integration layer for Technology to standardise.

📈

Value needs usage evidence

Licence value and adoption cannot be guessed. The team should track active use, repeat use, business scenarios, support issues and outcomes.

AI-specific scope

The AI platform stack this team owns

This is the missing middle between Business AI ideas and safe enterprise-scale adoption.

Employee AI Experience

Role-based AI journeys, approved examples, prompt patterns, training, champions and feedback loops.

1

Outcome

Employees know which AI tool to use, what good looks like, and where to get help.

AI Product Catalogue

Approved AI tools, Copilot experiences, plugins, agents, AI dev assistants and usage conditions.

2

Outcome

One front door for trusted AI instead of tool sprawl and confusing choices.

Prompt & Task Pattern Library

Reusable patterns for summarise, compare, draft, classify, retrieve, analyse, route and automate.

3

Outcome

People stop learning AI from random internet tips and use enterprise-safe patterns.

Agent Factory

Design, build, test, publish, monitor and retire agents using repeatable patterns.

4

Outcome

Agents become managed digital products, not one-off experiments.

Knowledge & Grounding Layer

Content quality, authoritative sources, SharePoint/Graph readiness, RAG patterns and connector choices.

5

Outcome

AI answers are grounded in trusted enterprise knowledge, not stale or random content.

Integration & MCP Layer

Patterns for connecting AI to apps, data, tools and workflows through connectors, APIs and MCP-style services.

6

Outcome

AI can act across systems with standard integration controls and clear ownership.

AI Evaluation & Quality

Golden test sets, hallucination checks, answer quality scoring, user feedback and release gates.

7

Outcome

The organisation can tell whether an AI experience is useful, safe and ready to scale.

AI Service Operations

AI help desk, incident categories, incorrect-answer triage, access issues, KBs and escalation paths.

8

Outcome

AI becomes supportable like any other enterprise technology service.

Practical use cases

Where the team creates real AI value

The team does not chase every business idea. It creates reusable AI capabilities that many teams can consume.

🧭

AI front door

Employees get a simple route to ask: which AI tool can I use, what is approved, and how do I request an agent?

CatalogueIntake
💬

Prompt pattern library

Ready-to-use examples for common work: summarise a meeting, compare options, write a brief, analyse a table, create actions.

PromptsProductivity
🤖

Agent factory

Build reusable agents for IT help, onboarding, policy Q&A, knowledge search, project handover and workflow triage.

AgentsCopilot Studio
📚

Knowledge grounding

Identify trusted sources, clean stale pages, map knowledge owners and make internal content AI-ready.

KnowledgeRAG
🔌

Connector and MCP patterns

Define how AI connects to enterprise apps, data, tools and workflows using approved integration patterns.

MCPConnectors
🧪

AI quality testing

Test prompts, agents and answers before scale. Track hallucination, poor retrieval, weak grounding and user feedback.

EvaluationQuality
🎓

Role-based adoption

Create targeted AI journeys for service desk, engineers, project teams, leaders and knowledge workers.

AdoptionEX
🎧

AI support desk

Handle AI-specific tickets: wrong answer, missing data, unclear source, access issue, failed agent, prompt misuse.

SupportITSM
📊

Usage and value dashboard

Show adoption, licence use, high-value scenarios, support friction, agent health and evidence readiness.

TelemetryValue
Scope boundary

Business AI vs AI Experience & Platform Enabler Team

This keeps the proposal clean: Business AI owns value. Technology owns the reusable AI platform and employee experience.

AreaBusiness AI teamAI Experience & Platform Enabler Team
MissionFind business opportunities, prioritise use cases and sponsor value.Make employee-facing AI usable, reusable, supportable and scalable.
OwnsBusiness outcomes, process redesign, ROI assumptions and sponsor engagement.AI catalogue, Copilot patterns, agents, prompts, knowledge grounding, integrations, support, evaluation and telemetry.
Primary customerBusiness functions and process owners.Employees, service teams, product owners, platform teams and operations.
Success measureBusiness value and transformation outcomes.AI adoption quality, reuse, supportability, answer quality, platform health, licence value and evidence.
Does not ownEnterprise platform operations and AI service management.Final business case ownership or business process accountability.
Operating model

Six capability lanes

Security and governance are included, but the core is AI enablement and platform operations.

AI Experience
& Platform
Enabler Team
PeopleRole-based adoption, champions, prompt coaching
PlatformCopilot, AI tools, agent platform, connectors
ServiceAI help desk, KB, escalation, known issues
ValueUsage, licence value, experience, outcomes
AgentsDesign, build, test, publish, monitor, retire
QualityEvaluation, grounding, answer review, feedback

AI Experience Lead

Owns employee journeys, guidance, prompt patterns and champions.

AI Platform Architect

Owns Copilot, agents, connectors, integration patterns and platform roadmap.

Agent Product Owner

Owns agent catalogue, intake, lifecycle, testing and release readiness.

Knowledge & Evaluation Lead

Owns grounding quality, content readiness, answer testing and feedback loops.

AI Service Operations Lead

Owns AI support model, KB, service desk training and incident workflow.

Security/Data Partner

Provides guardrails, access, data protection and assurance support.

Delivery workflow

From AI idea to reusable service

This workflow makes AI delivery practical: not endless governance, not uncontrolled experimentation.

1

Ask

Employee or team asks for AI help, agent, prompt pattern or tool access.

Output: request logged
2

Triage

Check business owner, user group, urgency, likely tool and reuse potential.

Output: route decided
3

Design

Define task, persona, data source, prompt, workflow, tool/action and success measure.

Output: AI design note
4

Build

Configure Copilot, agent, prompt, connector, workflow or knowledge source.

Output: working prototype
5

Evaluate

Test answer quality, grounding, edge cases, access and user experience.

Output: test evidence
6

Publish

Release to catalogue, train users, update KB and support route.

Output: reusable AI service
7

Improve

Review feedback, usage, support tickets, quality scores and changes needed.

Output: monthly improvement backlog
Everyday team work

What the team actually does each week

This makes the function tangible. It is not theory, and it is not just governance.

Monday

AI demand triage

  • Review new AI requests
  • Prioritise reusable patterns
  • Identify blockers

Tuesday

Build and configure

  • Agents
  • Prompt packs
  • Connectors
  • Knowledge sources

Wednesday

Evaluate quality

  • Test answers
  • Review hallucination risk
  • Improve grounding

Thursday

Enable users

  • Run clinics
  • Update guides
  • Train service desk

Friday

Measure and improve

  • Usage and feedback
  • Agent health
  • Next backlog
90-day evidence sprint

Start small, prove value, then scale

The first 90 days should prove whether the team is needed, what work is real, and what operating model is justified.

Days 1–30: Baseline and design

  • AI tool and agent inventory
  • Employee AI pain-point interviews
  • Copilot / AI usage baseline
  • Top 10 work patterns identified
  • Draft AI catalogue and intake route

Days 31–60: Build and enable

  • Publish first prompt pattern library
  • Stand up AI support categories and KB
  • Build 2–3 reusable agent prototypes
  • Define connector/MCP design principles
  • Run first AI clinics / champions session

Days 61–90: Measure and decide

  • Run agent and prompt quality tests
  • Publish dashboard: usage, support, adoption
  • Produce first evidence pack
  • Show outcomes from pilot teams
  • Recommend scale / adjust / stop decision
Leadership outcomes

What leaders get from this team

🧭

Clear AI front door

Employees know where to go for AI tools, agents, guidance and support.

🚀

Faster safe adoption

AI moves from random pilots to reusable workplace capabilities.

🧪

Better AI quality

Agents and prompts are tested, improved and grounded before wider rollout.

📊

Visible value

Leaders see adoption, usage, quality, licence value, support and evidence in one view.

Evidence boundary

Fact-based source notes

The page avoids unsupported vendor-style claims. It uses official or primary sources where possible.

  • Microsoft Copilot Studio documentation describes agent topics, knowledge, tools, prompts, agent flows and workflows.
  • Microsoft 365 Copilot extensibility documentation covers agents, connectors, plugins and custom Copilot experiences.
  • Official MCP documentation defines MCP as an open standard for connecting AI applications to external systems, tools, data sources and workflows.
  • Microsoft secure/govern Copilot guidance is used only as the control foundation, not as the whole operating model.
  • NIST AI RMF is used for risk-management structure: Govern, Map, Measure, Manage.
  • ISO/IEC 42001 is used as the AI management-system framing.
  • Australian public AI guidance is used as a maturity signal for accountable AI adoption and assurance.
Recommended leadership ask: approve a 90-day mandate to establish the AI Experience & Platform Enabler capability as a virtual Technology team and prove demand, value and operating model before formal headcount decisions.
